posts.author: Ghonarbochon
posts.text: Whats the relation of promoter integrity with promoters wife’s accidental death ?! Thats plain irrelevant ! Also , credit ratings companies charge money to issue the ratings and even listed companies stop furnishing data ( to avoid the effort needed in collecting the data) ,when they are no longer looking to raise cash and hence do not need the rating . That itself does not mean that the promoters are hiding bad stuff .If they had already decided to shutdown the business, its very normal to stop doing things and let the lapses happen .Until last year , winding down a company officially took a…

posts.author: Shail1234
posts.text: Absolutely, its like lesser evil. In sme which have to test waters finding right management is key.

posts.author: Prabhat_Mohanty
posts.text: Thanks Nabendu for the updates. Public memory is very short. That is the reason for many unscrupulous elements taking advantage of this by defrauding people. On any public uproar they lie low for sometime, but eventually return to their elements. And stock scams are a regular feature in our country because of our legal system and bureaucracy. For investors like me who have more than 30 years experience in investing in stocks, there are numerous example. In fact I have scores of physical share certificates of companies who have simply vanished. So try to learn lessons. In fact there are only fe…

posts.author: Nabendu
posts.text: @Amnesiac Neetu Yoshi . What I do not like with these stories is how conveniently these companies hide their past and probably do not mention it anywhere in public domain . Neetu Yoshi found by dynamic 2nd generation founders is the story told . But unfortunately the original promoter is their father Yogesh Lohia from JAGDAMBA LIQUIFIED STEELS LIMITED connect.acuite.in Press Release Now to whatever I can recollect and if I am not mistaken Neetu Yoshi was formed on the same land parcel and leveraged the same premises of JAGDAMBA LIQUIFIED STEELS LIMITED Lets see the google reviews and some post…

posts.text: Hello Nabendu, What makes you include Neetu Yoshi in the list of bad track record of IPOs for Horizon Management? Do you see any CG issues with Neetu Yoshi? Thanks.
